WARNING: These test points are for A2918 (820-02757) ONLY. For other boards, use that board's diagnostic files. Component designators and test points are board-specific — the same rail appears at DIFFERENT components on other boards.
description: Decision trees for all 20V DCPS scenarios. Board connected via USB-C at 20V (simulating post-PD negotiation or direct DCPS). This is the primary diagnostic voltage.

scenarios.meaning: Board draws almost nothing at 20V. Charger circuit is not loading. Either CD3217 is dead, charger IC is not accepting input, or PPBUS_AON has an open circuit.

steps.action: Verify DCPS is actually delivering 20V to the board
steps.if_missing: DCPS cable fault, connector fault, or USB-C flex disconnected.

steps.action: Check CD3217 (UF400) for signs of life
steps.measure: Probe PP1V05_UPC0_LDO_CORE (internal LDO output of UF400, page 71)
steps.if_missing: UF400 dead. Check for short on UF400 supply pins.
Charger IC (U5200 area) not converting input. Check CHGR_AUX_OK signal, I2C communication to charger.
steps.if_present: PPBUS_AON present but no downstream draw → go to step 4.

steps.if_missing: U5700 not starting. Check PPVAON_PWR_EN. Check delay circuit (D5340/R5340/R5341).
steps.if_present: PP3V8_AON present but 0A draw → PMU not loading. Check U7700/U8100.

scenarios.range: 0.05-0.5A (low current — partial activity, likely short holding board down)
scenarios.meaning: Board drawing some current but well below normal boot range (1.5-3A). Either a shorted rail is clamping output, or board is stuck in very early boot state.

steps.measure: Sequential probe: TPU633 → TPU630 → TPU636 → MPMU LDOs

sub_checks.if_wrong: MPMU LDO 9 fault — go to step 4
MPMU LDO 9B fault — go to step 4
MPMU LDO 13 fault — go to step 4

steps.if_all_present: All main rails present. Fault is in SoC domain or peripheral. Go to step 5.

steps.action: PP3V8_AON missing — investigate U5700 / short on 3V8 line
steps.measure: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON distribution caps and branches
steps.if_shorted: PP3V8_AON shorted. Thermal cam. Check VDDMAIN, WLBT, HDMI, speaker branches.
steps.if_not_shorted: U5700 enable fault. Check PPVAON_PWR_EN and delay circuit.

steps.measure: Check UC260 input, output passives, inductor
steps.if_ok: UC260 dead or PPV5S2TS_PWR_EN not asserted.

steps.action: MPMU LDO output missing — thermal cam on that rail
steps.measure: Thermal camera focused on the shorted/missing LDO rail
steps.expected: Hot component visible on the rail trace

steps.action: All rails present — check SoC and I2C buses
steps.measure: Check SMC I2C buses (pages 54-55), SoC reset signals
steps.if_wrong: I2C bus stuck → pull-up fault or peripheral holding bus low.

scenarios.range: 0.5-1.5A (partial boot — board powering up but stalling)
scenarios.meaning: Board is drawing boot-level current but not completing boot. SoC is likely powered but stalling during firmware load, display init, or storage access.

steps.action: Observe current pattern — stable or cycling?
steps.expected_cycling: Current rises to 1-2A, drops to 0, repeats (boot loop)

steps.measure: Check NAND power rails: PP1V25_MAME_NAND_VCC (SPMU BUCK 6), PP3V8_MAME_NAND_VDDL (SPMU BUCK 12 SW6/7)
steps.if_missing: NAND power fault. Check SPMU BUCK 6/12 outputs, FL4000/FL4001 fuses.
steps.if_present: NAND powered but SSD may be dead or firmware corrupted. Try DFU restore.

steps.if_missing: Display power path fault. Check UP710 sequencer, backlight regulators.

steps.measure: Probe PPVDD_MAME_PCPU, PPVDD_MAME_ECPU, PPVDD_MAME_GPU decoupling caps
steps.expected: Voltage present on all DVFS domains
steps.if_any_missing: SoC power domain fault. Cross-reference with power-path.json stage 7.
steps.if_all_present: SoC powered, display powered, stalled → likely firmware/SSD issue. DFU restore.

scenarios.meaning: This is the NORMAL operating range for an M3 MacBook Pro at 20V. 1.5-2.5A is typical boot/idle. 2.5-3A is under load. If the customer reports a fault and the board draws normal current, the fault is likely peripheral not power-related.

steps.action: Board is booting — check for display output
steps.measure: Look for Apple logo on display, backlight, chime
steps.if_display_working: Board boots. Fault is not power-related. Check: keyboard, trackpad, speakers, USB-C ports, HDMI output, SD card.

steps.action: No display but normal current — check display chain

sub_checks.if_no: Backlight regulator or UP710 sequencer fault.

sub_checks.if_yes: LCD panel or LDP flex fault. Replace display.
SoC GPU output fault. Check PPVDD_MAME_GPU.

steps.action: Specific peripheral faults at normal current

common_faults.check: UF400 or UF500 (whichever port). Test both ports individually.
Charger IC (U5200), CHGR_AUX_OK signal, battery connector (J5151).

scenarios.meaning: Excessive current draw. Something is either dead-shorted or the board is drawing max power.

steps.action: Is the board functional at this current?
steps.measure: Check if board boots and runs
steps.if_functional: Board may be under heavy load (charging + running). Monitor — should settle to 1.5-2.5A.

steps.action: Identify which power stage is pulling excess current
steps.measure: Thermal camera across entire board — look for hot spots
steps.if_found: Identify component → cross-reference with rails.json → replace.

steps.measure: Diode mode on major rails in order: PPBUS_AON (TPU633) → PP3V8_AON (TPU630) → PP5V_S2 (TPU636) → PP3V3_S2_LDO
steps.process: Find which rail reads short in diode mode. That's the faulted stage. Thermal cam that specific area.
steps.note: Pro-specific: also check PP3V8_AON_HDMI and PP3V3_MAME_S60 (SD card) branches which don't exist on Air models.
